chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA Crl.B.A.No.S-176 of 2024 (Ghulam Sarwar Vs. The State) DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For the hearing of the bail application. 04.07.2024. Mr. Ahmed Bux Abro, Advocate for the applicant. Mr. Aitbar Ali Bullo, D.P.G for the State. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- IRSHAD ALI SHAH, J;- It is alleged that the applicant with the rest of the culprits, caused hatchet blows to complainant Ghulam Ali, PWs Athar Ali, and Mumtaz Ali intending to commit their murder and then went away by insulting and threatening them of murder, for which the present case was registered. 2. The applicant on refusal of bail by learned 1st Additional Sessions Judge, Shahdadkot, has sought the same from this Court by way of instant application u/s. 497 Cr. PC. 3. Heard arguments and perused the record. 4. The FIR of the incident has been lodged with a delay of more than two months; such delay could not be overlooked. The injuries which the applicant has allegedly caused to PW Athar Ali, are not falling within the prohibitory clause. The parties are already disputed over the landed property. The case has finally been challaned and there is no likelihood of absconsion or tampering with the evidence on the part of the applicant. In these circumstances, a case for the release of the applicant on bail on point of further inquiry is made out. 5. Under the discussed circumstances, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court. 6. The instant bail application is disposed of accordingly.

Under the discussed circumstances, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court. 6. The instant bail application is disposed of accordingly. JUDGE
